Brand Style Guide & Design System — v1.4

TICON
Africa

The definitive reference for digital identity, user interface standards, and brand expression for the Technology and ICT Council of Africa — Africa's pan-continental ICT professional body.

Version
1.4
Audience
Design & Development Teams
Standard
WCAG 2.1 AA Compliant
Website
ticonafrica.org
01

Brand Foundation

TICON Africa exists as the connective tissue of a continent's digital future. Every visual and experiential decision must reflect the clarity, credibility, and continental ambition that define this organisation's positioning.

Positioning Pillars

🌍
Unity with Intent

Connect Africa's ICT professional bodies and practitioners to create a cohesive, collaborative network where shared purpose strengthens capability across the continent.

🏅
Standards with Credibility

Establish consistent, trusted professional benchmarks that elevate competence, reinforce ethical practice, and enable careers to move confidently across borders.

💡
Knowledge into Capability

Turn research, education, and specialised chapters into practical advancement that equips Africa's digital workforce to lead, innovate, and compete globally.

Brand Character

TICON Africa is authoritative without being inaccessible, African without being parochial, and professional without being sterile. The brand voice and visual identity must reflect this balance at every touchpoint.

DimensionTICON Africa IsTICON Africa Is Not
ToneConfident, clear, inclusiveBureaucratic, corporate-cold, or jargon-heavy
VisualBold, grounded, purposefulGeneric, trendy, or overly ornamental
StancePan-African, forward-lookingRegionally narrow, or imitative of Western tech brands
CredibilityEvidence-based, structuredPromotional, aspirational-without-substance
02

Logo System

The TICON Africa logo is the primary identifier of the organisation. It must be reproduced accurately, with adequate clear space, and only in approved colour configurations.

Clear Space Rule

The minimum clear space around the logo on all sides is equal to the height of the "T" character in the wordmark. This space must remain free of all other graphic elements, text, or imagery.

Primary Dark Background
Use on #0e4634 · Standard digital application
Dark / Reversed
Use on very dark or photographic backgrounds
Icon Mark — Light Bg
Use when wordmark is not required — favicons, app icons, social media profiles
Icon Mark — Dark Bg
Reversed icon for dark headers, footers, and branded backgrounds
ℹ The TICON Africa logo mark is a stylised, geometric representation of the African continent with a network node-and-line overlay — symbolising connectivity and the digital network spanning the continent. The orbital ring encircling the continent communicates global reach and technological ambition. Neither element may be separated from the other in the full lockup, nor redrawn, recoloured, or modified.

Approved Colour Configurations

ConfigurationWordmark ColourBackgroundUsage Context
Primary (Default)#0e4634 + #23b057 accent#FDFDFD / WhiteAll standard digital use
Reversed#FDFDFD + #23b057 accent#0e4634Dark headers, footers, branded backgrounds
Mono — Dark#0e4634White or light tintsSingle-colour printing, stamps
Mono — Light#FDFDFDDark or photographic backgroundsEmbossing, photography overlays

Logo Don'ts

✓ Always
  • Use the approved colour configurations only
  • Maintain required clear space on all sides
  • Use vector source files for all digital and print applications
  • Ensure the logo is legible at all reproduction sizes
  • Apply the logo on WCAG-compliant background contrasts
✕ Never
  • Alter the logo's proportions, colours, or letterforms
  • Apply drop shadows, outlines, or decorative effects
  • Place the logo on backgrounds that reduce legibility
  • Rotate, skew, or distort the logo in any way
  • Use the logo at sizes smaller than 80px wide (digital) or 25mm (print)
03

Colour System

The TICON Africa colour palette is rooted in the deep forest greens of the African landscape — grounded, verdant, alive. Each colour has a specific role, and accessibility constraints govern how colours may be combined for text and interactive elements.

♿ WCAG AA Commitment: All text–background colour combinations used in UI components, body copy, and interactive states must meet a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold). Decorative and non-informational elements are exempt. Contrast ratios are documented below each palette entry.

Primary Palette

WCAG AA ✓
Forest — Primary
#0e4634
Main brand colour. Use for headings, primary buttons, borders, and structural UI elements. White text on this colour: ~13.6:1. This colour on white: ~13.6:1. Both pass WCAG AA and AAA.
WCAG AA ✓*
Savannah — Secondary
#23b057
Active states, success indicators, highlighted CTAs. Use #0e4634 (dark) text only — contrast ratio 4.84:1 ✓. White text on this colour fails (2.82:1). Do not use for body copy on white backgrounds.
Background
Off-White — Background
#FDFDFD
Primary page background. Avoids the harshness of pure white while preserving perceived brightness. Always pair with #0e4634 text for accessible contrast.
WCAG AA ✓*
Kente — Accent
#ea7b21
Highlights, CTAs, progress indicators, notification dots, and decorative accents. Use #0e4634 (dark) text only — contrast 4.78:1 ✓. White text fails (2.85:1). Use sparingly — maximum one accent per page view.
Large Text
Granite — Neutral
#808285
Secondary text, captions, meta labels, and dividers. Contrast on white: ~3.89:1 — passes for large text (18pt+) only. Do not use for body copy or interactive labels. Use #3a3d40 for smaller neutral text.

Extended UI Tints

Primary Light
#1a6b51
Hover states on primary
Primary Dark
#092e23
Active / pressed states, footers
Secondary Pale
#e8f8ee
Success backgrounds, tag fills
Accent Pale
#fef3eb
Warning backgrounds, accent chips
Neutral Pale
#f0f0f0
Disabled states, code backgrounds

Contrast Ratio Reference — WCAG AA

ForegroundBackgroundRatioNormal TextLarge TextUse Case
#0e4634#FDFDFD13.6:1PassPassBody text, headings, labels
#FFFFFF#0e463413.6:1PassPassButton labels, nav links on primary
#0e4634#23b0574.84:1PassPassLabels on secondary green elements
#0e4634#ea7b214.78:1PassPassLabels on accent orange elements
#FFFFFF#23b0572.82:1FailFail❌ Do not use white text on secondary green
#808285#FDFDFD3.89:1FailPassCaptions only (18pt+). Not for body copy.
#3a3d40#FDFDFD9.5:1PassPassBody text substitute for neutral tone
#FFFFFF#ea7b212.85:1FailFail❌ Do not use white text on accent orange
04

Typography

Typography is the primary carrier of TICON Africa's voice. The pairing of Lemon Milk's geometric authority with Roboto's open readability reflects the union of bold African ambition with accessible, practical professionalism.

⚙ Lemon Milk — Heading Font: Lemon Milk is a commercial typeface by Marsnev and is not available on Google Fonts. It must be self-hosted or licensed via a web font service. Include the WOFF2 and WOFF formats in the project's /fonts/ directory and declare via @font-face. Fallback stack: 'Montserrat', 'Nunito', sans-serif.

Font Families

RoleFamilySourceWeights Used
Headings, DisplayLemon MilkSelf-hosted (marsnev.com)Regular (400), Bold (700)
Body, UI, LabelsRobotoGoogle Fonts CDNLight (300), Regular (400), Medium (500), Bold (700)
Code, MonospaceRoboto MonoGoogle Fonts CDNRegular (400), Medium (500)

Type Scale

Display
56px / 60px / Lemon Milk
Africa's Digital Future
H1 — Page Title
40px / 44px / Lemon Milk
Membership Benefits
H2 — Section Title
28px / 34px / Lemon Milk
Professional Standards
H3 — Sub-section
20px / 26px / Lemon Milk
Technical Working Groups
H4 — Component Heading
16px / 22px / Roboto 700
Regional Chapter — East Africa
Body Large
18px / 28px / Roboto 400
TICON Africa advances the professional standing of ICT practitioners across the continent through unified standards and shared knowledge.
Body Default
16px / 26px / Roboto 400
Members gain access to professional benchmarks, certification pathways, cross-border career recognition, and a network of over 20 national ICT associations.
Body Small
14px / 22px / Roboto 400
Event details, form helper text, card metadata. Minimum body size permitted in UI. Never use smaller than 14px for informational text.
Caption / Label
12px / 18px / Roboto 500
Category Label · Meta Info · Tags

Letter Spacing Rules

ContextTrackingNote
Lemon Milk Headings0.04em – 0.08emAlways use positive tracking; never negative. Larger sizes need less tracking.
Uppercase Labels0.12em – 0.2emAll-caps text at small sizes requires wider tracking for readability.
Body Copy0 (default)Do not alter tracking on Roboto body text.
Navigation Links0.02em – 0.04emSubtle tracking aids scannability.

Do's & Don'ts — Typography

✓ Always
  • Set body text at 16px minimum on screen
  • Use Lemon Milk for headings and display text only
  • Use Roboto for all body copy and UI labels
  • Maintain line length between 60–80 characters for paragraphs
  • Apply positive letter spacing to Lemon Milk headings
  • Ensure sufficient contrast between text and background (see Section 03)
✕ Never
  • Use Lemon Milk for body copy or paragraphs
  • Set text smaller than 12px in any UI context
  • Use decorative fonts for informational or instructional content
  • Justify body text (creates uneven word spacing)
  • Use more than two font families on a single page
  • Apply light (300) weight Roboto for text under 16px
05

Spacing & Grid

All spacing is derived from an 8px base unit. Consistent spatial rhythm reduces cognitive load and creates visual harmony across all UI contexts.

sm

Page Grid System

BreakpointMin WidthColumnsGutterMarginMax Content Width
Mobile (sm)320px416px16px100%
Tablet (md)768px824px32px100%
Desktop (lg)1024px1224px48px1280px
Wide (xl)1280px1232px80px1440px

Border Radius

sm
4px
md
8px
lg
16px
pill
100px

Use 4px for form inputs and buttons; 8px for chips, badges; 16px for cards and modals; pill for tags and status indicators only.

06

Buttons

Buttons are the primary mechanisms for user action. Hierarchy is critical — each page or view should have one primary action, and supporting actions should be visually subordinate.

Button Hierarchy

Full hierarchy — left to right: decreasing emphasis

Small Default Large

On Dark Backgrounds

Inverted context

PropertySmallDefaultLarge

Font Size13px14px16px
Font Weight500500500
Border Radius4px4px4px
Border Width2px2px2px
Focus Ring3px offset, var(--secondary) colour
Min Touch Target44×44px (WCAG 2.5.5)
07

Forms

Forms are the primary gateway for member registration, certification applications, and event sign-ups. They must be clear, forgiving, and accessible at every step.

Member Registration — Example
Use your professional or institutional email address
Please enter a valid profession (letters only)
✓ Emails match
Create Member Account

Form Field States

StateBorderBackgroundLabel
Default1.5px #d0d0ce#FFFFFF#0e4634
Hover1.5px #808285#FFFFFF#0e4634
Focus1.5px #0e4634 + 3px ring#FFFFFF#0e4634
Filled1.5px #0e4634#FFFFFF#0e4634
Error1.5px #c0392b + 3px ring#FFFFFF#c0392b
Disabled1.5px #d0d0ce#f4f4f2#808285

Form Accessibility Rules

✓ Required
  • Every input must have a visible, associated <label>
  • Required fields marked with an asterisk AND aria-required="true"
  • Error messages linked to inputs via aria-describedby
  • Autocomplete attributes on all personal data fields
  • Logical tab order through all focusable elements
  • In-line, persistent error messages (not just on blur)
✕ Prohibited
  • Placeholder text as a substitute for labels
  • Clearing input values on validation failure
  • Error messages as tooltip-only (must be persistent)
  • Colour alone to indicate field errors
  • CAPTCHAs without an audio or accessible alternative
  • Time-limited sessions without user warning
08

Cards

Cards contain bounded pieces of content — events, publications, member spotlights, and chapter information. The accent bar at the card's top signals content type.

Event · Nairobi
East Africa ICT Summit 2025

Bringing together practitioners, policymakers, and innovators across the Eastern corridor for three days of dialogue and collaboration.

14–16 November 2025 Register →
Publication · Research
State of ICT Workforce Africa 2024

Annual analysis of skills demand, salary benchmarks, and digital literacy indicators across 28 African nations.

Published Oct 2024 Download →
Certification · New
TICON Certified Digital Infrastructure Professional

Continental recognition for network and infrastructure specialists. Valid in all 54 member states.

Applications open Apply →

Card Accent Colour Semantics

Accent ColourContent Type
Primary — ForestEvents, announcements, general content
Secondary — SavannahPublications, reports, research, resources
Accent — KenteCertifications, opportunities, priority highlights
09

Navigation

Navigation is the primary wayfinding system. It must be consistent, always accessible, and reflect the structure of the professional community it serves.

TA
TICON Africa
About Membership Certifications Events Resources
Join Now

Navigation Specifications

ElementSpecification
Bar Height64px desktop, 56px tablet, 56px mobile (with hamburger)
Background#0e4634 (Primary) — sticky on scroll
Link TextRoboto 500, 13px, rgba(255,255,255,0.7) inactive
Active Indicator3px bottom border, #23b057
Primary CTABackground #23b057, text #0e4634 (contrast 4.84:1 ✓)
Skip LinkVisually hidden until focused; anchors to #main-content
MobileHamburger menu (≥44px touch target); drawer overlays from right
♿ Navigation must include a Skip to Content link as the first focusable element on every page. All dropdown menus must be keyboard navigable (arrow keys, Escape to close) and use correct ARIA roles: role="navigation", aria-label="Main navigation", aria-current="page" on active links.
10

Badges & Alerts

Badges label and categorise. Alerts communicate time-sensitive status. Both rely on colour + iconography + text to ensure accessibility for users with colour vision deficiencies.

Badges

Accredited Active Member Featured Archived Regional Chapter

System Alerts

✓
Registration Confirmed
Your application for the West Africa Chapter has been received. You will receive a confirmation email within 2 business days.
ℹ
Renewal Due in 30 Days
Your TICON Africa membership expires on 31 March 2026. Renew now to maintain access to all member benefits.
⚠
Document Upload Required
Your certification application is incomplete. Please upload your supporting credentials to proceed.
✕
Payment Failed
We could not process your payment. Please check your card details and try again, or contact support.
♿ All alerts must convey status through colour + icon + text — never colour alone. Use role="alert" for error and warning messages to trigger immediate screen reader announcement. Use role="status" for success and informational messages.
11

Iconography

Icons support navigation, labelling, and communication — never decoration alone. TICON Africa uses a consistent outlined icon style at 24×24px as the default.

Icon Usage Rules

ContextSizeColour
Navigation & UI actions20×20pxInherits from parent text colour
Button icons (leading)16×16pxSame as button label
Card and section indicators24×24pxPrimary (#0e4634) or Secondary (#23b057)
Alert icons20×20pxAlert semantic colour (see Section 10)
Decorative / illustration48px+Primary or Secondary palette only
Icon-only buttons20×20pxMust include aria-label (e.g., "Close dialog")

Recommended Icon Library: Use Heroicons (outlined style) or Phosphor Icons for consistency. All icon SVGs must include aria-hidden="true" when decorative, or role="img" with an aria-label when informational.

12

Imagery Guidelines

Photography and illustration communicate TICON Africa's continental identity and professional ambition. Every image must feel intentional, diverse, and contextually authentic.

Photography Direction

✓ Use
African ICT professionals in authentic work contexts — offices, data centres, field settings. Candid, purposeful, technically engaged.
✓ Use
Diverse representation across gender, age, geography, and ability. Pan-continental — not a single nation or region.
✕ Avoid
Generic stock photography of non-African contexts. Overly posed "suits in a boardroom" imagery. Any imagery that reads as tokenistic or stereotyping.
✕ Never
Low-resolution images below 72dpi digital or 300dpi print. Images without proper licensing. Photography with visible brand competitor logos.

Image Overlay — Accessibility

When placing text over photography, apply a scrim overlay using rgba(14, 70, 52, 0.65) minimum to ensure text contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1). All images used in content must include descriptive alt text. Decorative images must have alt="" to suppress screen reader announcement.

13

Motion & Animation

Motion at TICON Africa is purposeful and restrained. It confirms actions, guides attention, and provides spatial context — it does not entertain or distract.

Interaction Demos — Hover to see transitions

Fast
200ms
Hover / Active
Slow
500ms
Page Transitions

Animation Tokens

TokenValueUse Case
--duration-instant100msTooltips, indicator dots
--duration-fast200msHover states, button feedback, focus rings
--duration-normal300msDropdowns, accordions, modal open
--duration-slow500msPage entry, content reveals
--ease-standardcubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)General transitions
--ease-deceleratecubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1)Elements entering screen
--ease-acceleratecubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1)Elements leaving screen
♿ Reduced Motion: All animations must respect the prefers-reduced-motion: reduce media query. Wrap all non-essential transitions in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference). Essential feedback (focus rings, error states) must still be visible without animation.
14

Voice & Tone

TICON Africa's voice is that of a confident, collegial peer — not a distant institution. It speaks with authority earned through competence, not hierarchy.

Voice Attributes

AttributeIn PracticeNot This
AuthoritativeWe set professional standards for ICT in Africa.TICON Africa strives to hope to eventually set…
InclusiveWhether you're based in Cairo or Cape Town…All African members are required to…
DirectApply by 30 November. There are 14 places.Applications are encouraged ahead of the deadline.
HumanWe're proud of what this community has built.This organisation notes with satisfaction…

Tone Examples — UI Microcopy

✓ Error Message
We couldn't process your payment. Check your card details and try again — or contact us if the problem continues.
✕ Avoid
Error code 402. Transaction failure. Contact your financial institution.
✓ Empty State
No events yet in your region — but that's about to change. Browse all upcoming summits across the continent.
✕ Avoid
No results found.
✓ Confirmation Message
You're registered for the Lagos ICT Forum. We'll send details to your email three days before the event.
✕ Avoid
Registration successful. A confirmation email will be dispatched in due course.
15

Accessibility Standards

TICON Africa is committed to WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance across all digital touchpoints. Accessibility is not optional — it is a prerequisite for inclusion across the continent's diverse range of devices, connectivity levels, and abilities.

WCAG 2.1 AA — Core Principles

PrincipleCriteriaImplementation Requirement
Perceivable1.1.1 Non-text ContentAll images have descriptive alt text; decorative images have alt=""
1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum)4.5:1 for normal text; 3:1 for large text
1.4.11 Non-text ContrastUI components and state indicators: 3:1 against adjacent colours
Operable2.1.1 KeyboardAll functionality available via keyboard; no keyboard traps
2.4.3 Focus OrderFocus follows logical DOM order; skip links provided
2.4.7 Focus VisibleAll interactive elements show a 3px focus ring in #23b057
Understandable3.3.1 Error IdentificationErrors identified in text; suggestions provided where possible
3.3.2 Labels or InstructionsAll inputs have persistent visible labels
Robust4.1.2 Name, Role, ValueAll UI components use correct semantic HTML and ARIA attributes

Additional Accessibility Commitments

✓ Implement Always
  • Logical heading hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3, no skipping)
  • Semantic HTML first; ARIA only when native semantics insufficient
  • lang="en" (or appropriate language) on the HTML element
  • Keyboard-navigable dropdown menus and modals
  • Sufficient target size ≥ 44×44px for all interactive elements
  • Text resizable up to 200% without loss of functionality
  • prefers-reduced-motion media query honoured
✕ Prohibited
  • Relying on colour alone to convey meaning
  • Auto-playing media with sound (no user trigger)
  • Content that flashes more than 3 times per second
  • Opening links in new tabs without warning the user
  • Using tabindex values greater than 0
  • Removing native focus outlines without a custom replacement
🔧 Testing Protocol: Run automated checks with Axe or Lighthouse at every release. Supplement with manual keyboard-only navigation testing and screen reader testing using NVDA (Windows) and VoiceOver (macOS/iOS). Test with real users on low-bandwidth connections (simulate 3G) relevant to African market conditions.
16

UX Recommendations

The following recommendations target the existing ticonafrica.org website. They are designed to reduce cognitive load, improve conversion, and strengthen the professional impression of the brand — without altering its core visual identity.

📋 Priorities are classified as High (implement immediately), Medium (next sprint), or Low (roadmap item). All recommendations preserve the existing TICON Africa brand aesthetic.
01

Establish a single, unmistakable primary CTA per page High

Multiple competing call-to-action buttons on a single page increase decision fatigue. Define one primary action per page — typically "Join / Become a Member" on landing pages — and visually subordinate all other actions using secondary or ghost button styles. This immediately reduces cognitive load and improves click-through rates on the most important conversion path.

02

Add a persistent value proposition above the fold High

First-time visitors need to immediately understand what TICON Africa offers them personally. Implement a structured hero section with a single punchy headline (Lemon Milk, max 8 words), a benefit-led sub-headline in Roboto, and the three positioning pillars rendered as compact icon-text pairs. This replaces ambiguous introductory text with scannable value communication.

03

Simplify navigation to maximum 5 top-level items High

Navigation menus with more than 5–7 items require users to actively search rather than scan. Consolidate current navigation to: About / Membership / Certifications / Events / Resources. Use mega-menus or dropdown sub-menus for deeper content. This aligns with the "rule of 5" in information architecture and reduces the time users spend in the nav bar.

04

Introduce section-level visual hierarchy with whitespace Medium

Dense page layouts without spatial breathing room make content feel bureaucratic and overwhelming. Apply the 8px spacing system systematically — minimum 64px between major page sections on desktop, 48px on mobile. Use background alternation (white ↔ #f4f4f2) to visually group related content without adding borders or additional graphic elements.

05

Implement a sticky header with a condensed logo state Medium

As users scroll, the navigation should remain accessible without consuming screen real estate. On scroll past 80px, transition the header to a condensed 56px height, reduce logo to wordmark-only, and hide any secondary nav items behind a "More" disclosure button. This keeps core actions accessible on every page without the cognitive cost of re-orientation.

06

Introduce an Africa map as an interactive member community visual Medium

A lightweight, SVG-based map of Africa with member country indicators immediately communicates the continental scope of TICON Africa's network. Countries with active chapters could be highlighted in Secondary green; associate member countries in a lighter tint. This creates instant emotional connection with the brand's pan-African positioning — without adding any new brand colour outside the existing palette.

07

Standardise card usage across all content lists Medium

Events, publications, and certifications should share a consistent card component structure as defined in Section 08. This creates visual predictability — users learn once how to read a card and can apply that understanding across the entire site. Mixed presentation formats (some in cards, some in tables, some as link lists) increase the mental effort required to extract information.

08

Add progress indicators to multi-step processes Low

Membership applications, certification registrations, and event sign-ups likely involve multi-step forms. Without a visual progress indicator, users cannot gauge effort, increasing abandonment. Implement a linear progress stepper using Primary green for completed steps, Secondary green for the active step, and Neutral grey for upcoming steps — clearly labelled at each stage.

09

Optimise for low-bandwidth network conditions Low

A significant portion of TICON Africa's audience will access the site on mobile networks across the continent, including 2G and 3G connections. Implement lazy loading for below-the-fold images, use WebP format with JPEG fallback, set performance budgets (under 200KB initial page load), and test with Chrome DevTools at "Slow 3G" to ensure the core information architecture remains usable under constrained conditions.

10

Add a persistent search capability to the site Low

As content volume grows with events, certifications, publications, and member resources, a visible search function becomes essential to reduce navigation friction. Implement a search icon in the navigation bar that expands to a full-width search overlay. Use the brand's existing colour system for the search overlay (Primary dark background, Secondary green active state) to maintain brand consistency.

Cognitive Load Reduction — Quick Wins

PatternCurrent IssueFix
Text contrastGrey text on white may fall below 4.5:1Use #3a3d40 minimum for all body copy (9.5:1)
Line lengthFull-width paragraphs on wide screens (100+ chars)Cap content columns at 680px max-width
Heading hierarchyVisual size ≠ semantic level in some sectionsAudit and align visual + semantic heading order
Empty states"No results" with no guidanceAdd action-oriented empty state copy and a next-step link
Loading statesPage sections reflow without indicationAdd skeleton screens in Neutral Pale (#f0f0f0) for loading content
Mobile tap targetsSmall text links in navigation on mobileMinimum 44×44px touch area on all interactive elements
TICON Africa
Brand Style Guide & Design System — Version 1.4
All specifications subject to review and update by the TICON Africa Digital Committee.
WCAG 2.1 Level AA Compliant
